items.q: Most movements in the human body use which class of lever?
items.why: Students assume the most familiar (first class - seesaw) is most common. Actually most body levers are third class (effort between fulcrum and load) - like the biceps curl. They sacrifice mechanical advantage for speed/range.

items.q: What is the rough formula for maximum heart rate?
items.why: Students misremember the formula. 220 - age is the classic estimate (rough; varies individually). A 20-year-old's estimated max HR is around 200 bpm; an 80-year-old's is around 140.

items.correct: Gradually increasing training demands so the body keeps adapting.
items.misconception: Training as hard as possible immediately.
items.why: Students think 'overload' means 'maximum'. It means slightly exceeding current capacity - too much causes injury; too little stalls progress. The key word is PROGRESSIVE.
The answer examiners want is: Sagittal plane. “Frontal plane” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A pirouette rotation occurs about the:
items.why: Correct answer: Longitudinal axis. “Frontal axis” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: If a gymnast does 2 full rotations on the longitudinal axis, total angular displacement (rad) is?
items.why: The real figure is 12.57. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
